sections.content:
Good. Now I can see:
- Before click: Node 0: P (pink), 56 pixels; Node 1: c (charcoal), 8 pixels
- After click: Node 0: P (pink), 55 pixels; Node 1: c (charcoal), 9 pixels
So clicking on the pink bar removes 1 pixel from the pink bar (56 -> 55), and creates 1 charcoal pixel (8 -> 9).
This is consistent with pattern: each click on the pink bar removes 1 pixel from the pink bar and creates 1 charcoal pixel.
So I need to click 55 times to remove all 55 pixels from the pink bar.
But the instructions say "Level 1: you have used 7 of about 7 target actions so far."
So I need about 7 actions to complete the level.
This doesn't add up.
Let me think about this differently.
Maybe the goal is not to click on the pink bar, but to click on something else.
Let me look at the board. The board has:
- A pink bar at the top, a gray background, a white area on the right, a black bar with yellow pixels in the middle, and blue squares on the right edge.
Maybe the goal is to click on the pink bar to remove it, and the charcoal pixels are just a byproduct.
But wait - the instructions say "Level 1: you have used 7 of about 7 target actions so far."
So I need about 7 actions to complete the level.
Let me try clicking on the pink bar again.
